review_title: The fast, the reliable and the universal database access components for Delphi/Lazarus
description: I have used UniDAC components in several applications and have gained experience accessing Firebird, Oracle, and Microsoft SQL Server databases. And what can I say: I’m a big fan of UniDAC. These days, one might well wonder whether you have to pay to establish a connection to a database system. In the Java environment, however, hardly anyone would do this or even need to. In the Delphi or Lazarus development environments, however, the situation is different. Yes, there are free solutions, but UniDAC still offers me added value, so — provided I’m not completely broke — I’ve decided to treat myself to a UniDAC license even as a hobbyist developer, rather than struggling with alternatives. And if, as a hobbyist developer, I already consider the advantages of UniDAC over other solutions is worth the money, then in a commercial software development context, I wouldn't even have to think twice about it.
author: Marian S.
pros: UniDAC gives me the freedom to develop Delphi applications that can connect to a wide variety of databases—and for some DB systems, this can even be done without installing additional database drivers. UniDAC offers excellent compatibility (from legacy versions like Delphi 7 to the latest release) and performance. Whenever a new major version of Delphi is released, a corresponding updated version of UniDAC is released shortly thereafter. The most compelling advantages for me compared to other solutions were the ease of setup, the broad support for IDEs and databases, the ability to access the database systems I use directly (without additional drivers)—and the speed of database access.
cons: I can’t really think of a direct downside. Of course, there’s the price... For someone who distributes software for free—whether out of enjoyment, for personal use, or simply to share their creations—the price is certainly a hurdle. Especially since there are free alternatives like ZeosLib.

review_title: I highly recommend Universal Data Access Components
description: Love it! I wish I would have started using it years earlier. Was very hesitant moving to a big boy database. Was used to dBase and the Absolute Database. Both worked but I needed to move to multi-million record tables. This does the job hands down.
author: David S.
pros: Was able to get started and do basic functionality very quickly. With on-line searching I've been able to accomplish everything I've needed to do.
cons: I wish there was more documentation. I see properties and procedures that I don't yet know what they do. I don't know if I even need them. Everything works as I think they should.

author: Thomas H.
pros: one of the few software solutions on the market that has done what it was designed to do without any problems and without errors right from the start. Microsft should take a look here ;-)
cons: TPgDump component does not provide functionality to backup only some columns/datafields of a table (postgres) and then to only restore these values without restoring all the other existing and possible newer and essential content.

description: The components are easy to deploy, allowing our software to be used with Firebird, Oracle, or MS-SQL without any programming changes. The components have functioned reliably even across multiple versions.
author: Rainer S.
pros: I've been using the components for many years. They're very stable and performant. New Delphi versions are also supported immediately. Customer service responds quickly and competently to any questions.
cons: I didn't notice anything that wasn't good.

review_title: Reliable Database Connection with UniDAC and Delphi
description: We use UniDAC in our company’s EMDI business management software. It has been reliable, fast, and makes it simple to connect with both Firebird and MySQL. I also really like the included dbMonitor tool, which makes it much easier to debug and trace database activity during development.
author: Solon Z.
pros: Works great with Firebird, which is our main database, and also with MySQL for our web apps. Easy to use and very stable.
cons: Error handling could be improved. In some cases the feedback is not detailed enough, which makes debugging harder and less efficient.

review_title: UniDAC is feasible upgrade option from older DB components
description: We use UniDAC for large business app with MsSQL and Firebird databases. UniDAC is highly advanced database component set for Delphi.
author: Marek D.
pros: UniDAC provides native driver for MsSQL - no need for Microsoft OLE DB drivers, wide variety of useful properties and events and good recovery after connection breakdown and reconnect.
cons: Fetch records management is hard to use with MsSQL. Opening query with unfetched records leads to "New transaction is not allowed because there are other threads running in the session" when another transaction tries to access same records. We programmed own requery management when user needs more records than initial load (similar to MaxRecords in ADO and client side cursor).

description: We are a small company and we mainly work with Firebird and MSSQL databases. We recently started using PostgreSQL and the conversion was surprisingly easy with UniDac.
author: Attila H.
pros: - easy install and use - excellent performance (Firebird, MSSQL) - easy to learn, simple development
cons: - nothing - Updates arrive relatively often, and we always wonder if they are necessary. We usually wait for less busy days and install them then, so far we have not had any compatibility issues.

description: We've been using it for more than 10 years. Awesome product, and will probably keep on using it for the foreseeable future.
author: Chris B.
pros: Ease of use and the myriad of databases to connect to instantly. Easy to install and almost no learning curve coming from a Delphi background using their components.
cons: Nothing. Everything is just fantastic! Like I said, if you are a Delphi programmer coming from the Borland BDE days and working with the standard database components, you'll find these extremely easy to integrate with.

review_title: Great set of data access components
description: We have been using this library for almost 20 years now, and found it reliable, easy-to-use, and frequently updated to support new database versions.
author: Yeoh R.
pros: Wide range of databases supported. Supports database-specific features. Good variety of components to support many use cases. Easy to use. Fantastic support.
cons: Have not encountered any major issues in our almost 20 years using this set of data access components.

review_title: Using for 10 years and has been the most reliable data tier since BDE.
description: We have been able to contact support and gotten a night build change to access Oracle when they changed to allow an off site redundant server configuration.
author: Tim S.
pros: The software has built in code to access database servers directly. For example, you can write a single EXE that can access an Oracle database WITHOUT Oracle intalled. They have the code for OCI built in. Yes, you can use OCI and OLE DB and ODBC and hit 20 other databases with the same code. Of course you can access MsSql the same way. What does this give you?? You can make an Android app that connects direct to a Mssql database over the internet. Maybe you will do REST services later, but quickly make an awesome app.
cons: I don't like that they didn't give me a job offer.

review_title: The VCL for several database access
author: Le Peuvedic H.
pros: Easy to use, look like the BDE ... Several database engine supported.
cons: No things wrong in this suite. All is ok... In the future May be a remote database tools for Json could be nice...

review_title: Finally a way to properly replace the BDE without relying on shaky OleDB support
author: Olivier S.
pros: It's a solution that allows us to connect to many databases without needing to use drivers that may be missing from the end user computer. Anyone who worked with Oracle knows how painful it can be to install their data access layer, Unidac allows us to never bother with this again.
cons: It requires replacing existing components, but it's close enough to them that a simple DFM editing is often enough.

description: I have used this software for many years and it's an essential part of my programming toolbox. I wouldn't be without it, and use it in preference to any other database component suites.
author: James G.
pros: Installation is simple; using the components is also easy. Documentation is good, and the ability to flip between database platforms by changing connectors is like magic. On the odd occasion I have needed support, the response has been helpful and timely. I have developed against SQL Server, MySQL, SQLite, Access, Oracle and Advantage database platforms with no issues at all. Another great feature is that there are no dlls that have to be distributed with an application.
cons: There isn't really anything to dislike!

author: Murat A.
pros: Direct Access, Performance, Providers, Using
cons: I didn't see anything wrong with the usage.

author: Luca D.
pros: This product is easy to use, integrates well with my applications, simplifies code writing and speeds it up.
cons: I found no negatives, since I installed it I have always used it

description: Great component easy to use, does not fail.
author: Franco A.
pros: Great stability, no necessary to use external libraries. We don't even need to install a database driver to function. Our deployment made easy.
cons: Everything works fine, we don't have any cons for now.

review_title: Software de acceso a base de datos
description: Lo utilizo en toda la aplicación para el acceso a la base de datos
author: Daniel V.
pros: Fácil de utilizar, con soporte muy bueno.
cons: Faltaría poder configurar un punto general para el formato de fecha.

description: overall good but it would be excellent if query response times were much faster
author: Eugenio C.
pros: the simplicity of access from the mobile app
cons: the too much time it takes to query large tables when I insert the order by clause

review_title: UNIDAC - Very good library for my Delphi projects
description: Excellent library for my Delphi projects.
author: Ondrej P.
pros: Highly unified access to different databases. Testing the results of SQL queries directly in the graphical design of Delphi.
cons: Limited macro options directly in SQL queries (to solve minor SQL incompatibilities)

author: Leonardt N.
pros: Besonders beeindruckend ist die enorme Zeitersparnis durch die konsistente API: Einmal geschriebener Code funktioniert ohne große Anpassungen für unterschiedlichste Datenbanksysteme. Die Stabilität der Komponenten ist auch bei hohen Datenlasten hervorragend, und die Integration in die IDE (Delphi/C++Builder) fühlt sich sehr nativ und flüssig an. Man merkt einfach, dass hier jahrelange Erfahrung in der Datenbankentwicklung steckt, was das Vertrauen in die eigenen Anwendungen stärkt.
cons: Gelegentlich wirken die Fehlermeldungen etwas generisch, was die Ursachenforschung bei Verbindungsproblemen manchmal zeitintensiver macht als nötig. Zudem wäre es wünschenswert, wenn neue Features oder Provider-Updates noch schneller in die Basis-Dokumentation einfließen würden.

review_title: O Universal Data Access Components é o melhor
description: Desenvolvo pequenos aplicativos em todos eu uso o unidac por gostar mesmo e pela simplicidade. O Universal Data Access Components são componentes sempre irei usar.
author: RONALDO L.
pros: O que eu gosta é praticidade de não ficar preocupado em ficar colocando dll na pasta do exe, muito bom, eu recomendo.
cons: Na minha opinião ainda não encontrei nada que não goste mais quando eu encontrar eu falo no suporte o que eles poderiam melhor.

description: I have been using Unidac for Delphi for over two decades to connect to Oracle, MySQL, NexusDB, SQL Server, PostgreSQL and Firebird databases. The product is stable, accesses databases with excellent performance and Devart's technical support is very good.
author: Samuel N.
pros: Excellent performance. Connection to multiple databases. Use of "macros" in SQL commands.
cons: Since it has a lot of options/settings, it took me a while to get to know and use all of its features.

description: The product is reliable and fast - I have not suffered any loss of data while accessing my data is fast.
author: Johan B.
pros: Easy and logical to use, and fits seamlessly into the Delphi development environment.
cons: Nothing really to dislike with a few limitations.

author: Vagelis B.
pros: Support of all database Schemas in the market
cons: Easy migration to other Devart Data Access Components

review_title: Devart Unidac: a very good product
description: I use those components since many years, and I never had a problem with them, even when upgrading to the next version. Thumbs up!
author: Lionello F.
pros: I find those components extremely stable, flexible and fast. Also the documentation is good.
cons: I would like a single installer for different versions of Delphi on the same machine.
